---
title: "EcoPostView: Ecological Posterior View"
author: Willem (Wim) Kaijser
output: 
  bookdown::gitbook:
    toc_depth: 4
    number_sections: true
    highlight: tango
---
<style>
.main-container {
  max-width: 100%;
}

body {
  text-align: justify;
}

.outline-block {
  border: 2px solid #333;
  padding: 1em;
  margin: 1em 0;
  border-radius: 6px;
  background-color: #fafafa;
}
</style>

```{r setup_1, include = FALSE}
library(EcoPostView)

#unlink("C:/Users/admin/AppData/Local/R/win-library/4.3/_build/00LOCK-EcoPostView", recursive = TRUE, force = TRUE)
#
#unlink("C:/Users/admin/OneDrive/Bureaublad/EcoPostView.Rcheck", recursive = TRUE, force = TRUE)
#
#dir.exists("C:/Users/admin/AppData/Local/R/win-library/4.3/_build/00LOCK-EcoPostView")
#dir.exists("C:/Users/admin/OneDrive/Bureaublad/EcoPostView.Rcheck")

#check stuff
#devtools::check(check_dir = "C:/temp/EcoPostView-check")
#
#build book
#devtools::document()
#devtools::install(upgrade = "never")
#
#create the book
#setwd("C:/Users/admin/OneDrive/Bureaublad/EcoPostView/book")
#bookdown::render_book("index.Rmd")

```

# Overview

This is a working site with multiple projects, each chapter serves as a project although connected. It serves as a way to organize my thoughts and collect fragments of information. The project originally started including philosophical basis, but I have omitted this. Each chapter now contains a project or miscellaneous functions and ideas and some heterodox-statistical theory at the end.

On the left, you will find these chapters in the drop down menu. They begin with an explorations of the EcoPostView package. This site is still a work in progress — a kind of evolving rather than a polished presentation.
